sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Persistent belly pain for several weeks.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a persistent pain in the right lower quadrant of his belly for several weeks. He describes the pain as a severe, squeezing sensation, accompanied by nausea and sickness. The pain is intermittent, but when it occurs, it's quite severe. He notes that the pain is consistently worse after eating and sometimes occurs two hours after meals as well. He has been following a keto diet to manage his type 2 diabetes, which he was diagnosed with several years ago. He reports that his diet was worse before his diagnosis, and since then he has cut back on sugar and started a keto diet, which has led to a 25-pound weight loss over the past three months. He finds it easier to manage his diabetes with the keto diet, but he still experiences the pain. He has a family history of gallbladder disease, with both his parents having had their gallbladders removed. He reports that he has had low-grade fevers and has not vomited.
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Physical exam reveals positive guarding in the right upper quadrant. Bowel sounds are present and normophonetic bilaterally. Vital signs are stable: blood pressure 128/88, respiratory rate 16, pulse 68, oxygen saturation 99% on room air. Murphy's sign is positive. The ultrasound revealed multiple gallstones in the gallbladder and mild thickening of the gallbladder wall.
Assessment
The patient presents with clinical signs consistent with cholecystitis secondary to gallstones. The patient's symptoms and exam findings are most consistent with acute cholecystitis.
Plan
I recommend a laparoscopic cholecystectomy to remove the gallbladder. I will order a series of radiographs to ensure that the procedure is safe. The patient will be admitted to the hospital for one night to monitor him post-procedure. The patient should avoid high-fat foods after surgery. Follow up in one week if symptoms persist. I will schedule a follow-up appointment with the patient in one week to discuss the results of the procedure and to discuss the next steps in his care. I will also schedule a follow-up appointment with the patient in one week to monitor his diabetes.
